mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is a Kubernetes operator that dynamically manages DNS records for Kubernetes resources such as Services and Ingresses. It is particularly useful for environments where services are frequently created or destroyed, as it automates the synchronization of DNS records with external DNS providers. Key trade-offs include the need for proper permissions and the potential for DNS propagation delays.

steps.title: Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

```

> This is the official Bitnami chart repository for ExternalDNS.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ExternalDNS in the 'kube-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

```

> Adjust the provider and zoneType according to your DNS provider.
commands: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

steps.title: Create an ExternalDNS Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) that defines how ExternalDNS should manage DNS records.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.kubernetes.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: example-external-dns
spec:
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  provider: aws
  policy: upsert-only
EOF

```

> Replace 'example.com' with your actual domain.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.kubernetes.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: example-external-dns
spec:
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  provider: aws
  policy: upsert-only
EOF

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ExternalDNS deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This step removes ExternalDNS from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all DNS records managed by ExternalDNS, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete customresourcedefinition externaldns.kubernetes.io

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This step will upgrade ExternalDNS to the latest version.

```bash

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> Ensure to specify the correct version you want to upgrade to.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create record set'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions for the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the IAM role or service account used by ExternalDNS has the necessary permissions to create DNS records.

troubleshooting.title: No DNS records created for services
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the services do not have the correct annotations.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your services have the appropriate annotations for ExternalDNS to manage them.

troubleshooting.title: ExternalDNS pod is in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate a misconfiguration in the ExternalDNS deployment.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the ExternalDNS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: DNS records not updating
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if there are network issues or if the DNS provider is experiencing downtime.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity to the DNS provider and verify its status.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new DNS providers and improved handling of DNS record updates.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pod running in the kube-system namespace and DNS records created in your specified DNS provider.
codeSnippets: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.kubernetes.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: example-external-dns
spec:
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  provider: aws
  policy: upsert-only
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
